summary_one_line: Input must carry the ICMP6 next-header byte 0x3a so GetIpProto() selects case kProtoIcmp6.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The parsed IPv6 next-header / protocol field must equal kProtoIcmp6 (0x3a = 58); winner seeds carry a run of 0x3a bytes around offsets 0x07-0x0e so GetIpProto() returns ICMP6 and the switch enters case kProtoIcmp6.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner (naive) seeds hold concentrated 0x3a (ASCII ':') bytes at offsets 0x07 and 0x08 (W bytes '3a(:)x3') and a 0x3a run through 0x0b-0x0d, the literal ICMP6 protocol value, so the true side at L1761 is taken (W: T=3 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser (cmplog) seeds carry 0x00 / 0x29 at offset 0x07 (L bytes '00(.)x7 29())x3') and never plant 0x3a, so GetIpProto() yields a non-ICMP6 value and the switch falls to default (L: T=0 F=32 at L1761, T=32 F=0 at default L1765).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is the I2S-free baseline (naive): its havoc/interesting-value stack lands the 0x3a protocol byte by plain byte mutation, and the I2S-free arm WINS here. cmplog's input-to-state operand substitution, anchored on CMP operands captured upstream, keeps planting non-ICMP6 protocol values and steers the queue toward the UDP/default arms, so the comparison-solving technique is the harmful delta and the surviving feature is reached by plain havoc-only byte mutation.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the ICMP6 arm of the GetIpProto() switch.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the 0x3a protocol byte at offsets 0x07 and 0x08; loser seeds do not.
Offset 0x08 is also concentrated to 0x3a in the winner and absent in the loser.
A winner-resolving seed contains the 0x3a run in its bytes.
On the call chain the blocker's ICMP6 case is taken only by the winner; the loser falls to default.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1761  T=3 F=0  T=0 F=32  case kProtoIcmp6:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair has naive as winner and cmplog as loser with delta I2S, so the I2S-free arm wins.
The winner naive fuzzer has no I2S mutator, only the havoc/token stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes CMP operands captured per-execution, which is the technique that differs from naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is havoc-only (winner = naive, the I2S-free baseline), so lineage I2S-floor verification is not applicable; this is an anti-I2S pair (naive > cmplog) where the I2S technique is the harmful delta, and the winning side is reached by naive havoc/interesting-value byte mutation planting the 0x3a protocol byte, corroborated by winner Seed 2 mutation_op TokenReplace,DwordInterestingMutator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where placing the 0x3a (kProtoIcmp6) protocol byte is the ONLY gate to the winning branch but cmplog still resolves it as fast as or faster than naive — that would show I2S is not the harmful delta and the win is not havoc-driven.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal claim that cmplog's I2SRandReplace actively steers the queue away from 0x3a (rather than cmplog merely being neutral/slower) is inferred from the divergence, not directly observed in operand logs.
